 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.1.4","title":"[AA-01.1] Define tail-latency decomposition contract and metric taxonomy","description":"Define the canonical tail equation for Asupersync and map each term to concrete runtime counters, snapshot fields, and structured-log keys across scheduler, cancellation, I/O, synchronization, retry, and allocation paths.","design":"Scope:\n- Standardize the decomposition: tail = queueing + service + io_or_network + retries + synchronization + allocator_or_cache.\n- Attach each term to precise producers such as wake-to-run delay, lane backlog, drain backlog, lock wait/hold time, retry inflation, and allocation churn.\n- Keep the schema deterministic and cheap enough for always-on core fields plus optional extended fields.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A versioned taxonomy exists with field definitions, units, and producer locations.
2. Runtime, benchmark, and deterministic E2E emitters can produce the core fields without ambiguity.
3. Unknown or currently unmeasured latency contributions fall into an explicit unknown bucket rather than disappearing.
4. Deterministic unit tests, golden-log checks, and workload-smoke E2E scripts protect field names, units, schema stability, and operator-facing attribution reports.","notes":"JadeGrove reduced work to doc-alignment support only after concurrent canonical implementation landed on src/observability/diagnostics.rs; no code ownership retained on the live AA-01.1 surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:49.490084743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:58:42.370082748Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T06:58:42.370059786Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa01-baseline","alien-artifact","measurement","performance","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:49.490084743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.1.5","title":"[AA-01.2] Build replayable workload corpus and reproducibility bundle","description":"Assemble the workload corpus that later tracks must use for before/after claims: CPU-heavy, cancellation-heavy, I/O-heavy, bursty, timer-heavy, fan-out/fan-in, and distributed-preview scenarios with deterministic manifests and replay commands.","design":"Scope:\n- Define representative scenario families and parameter grids.\n- Emit reproducibility bundles: manifests, env metadata, seed/config pointers, command lines, and expected evidence artifacts.\n- Make the corpus usable by benches, E2E scripts, and controller shadow runs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. -{"id":"asupersync-1508v.10","title":"[AA-10] Proof, CI, and Progressive-Delivery Closure Fabric","description":"Close the entire program with one auditable fabric for claim graphs, evidence bundles, CI gates, calibration drift detection, progressive delivery, and comparative sign-off.","design":"Why this track exists:
- Radical systems work dies without disciplined closure. The point is not merely to invent novel mechanisms, but to make them shippable, reviewable, reversible, and composable.
- This track turns all prior work into a durable operational package.

Track outputs:
- Claim/evidence/policy/trace graph schema.
- CI and rollout gates for proof, calibration, and tail behavior.
- Cross-track composition gauntlet with compatibility matrix and incident drill pack.
- Final comparative demos and launch doctrine.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every major runtime claim in the ascension program can be traced to evidence, policies, tests, and rollback triggers.
2. CI can fail closed on proof/artifact mismatch, calibration drift, tail-SLO regression, or missing validation-pack coverage.
3. Cross-track composition is validated explicitly, with supported versus experimental versus forbidden combinations documented for operators and future contributors.
4. Progressive delivery is encoded as doctrine and tooling, not tribal knowledge.
5. Closure artifacts are self-contained enough for future contributors to continue the program without reconstructing the original planning discussion.
6. The track produces deterministic E2E closure demos, structured release and incident logs, and exact rerun commands for every promoted claim or blocked promotion.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: make bold runtime work survivable in production engineering reality.\n- EV: maximal for long-term adoption because it governs whether the rest of the program can be trusted.\n- Primary risk: bureaucracy without signal.\n- Countermeasure: keep gates artifact-driven, comparator-driven, and directly tied to claimed wins or safety properties.\n- Conservative fallback: if any advanced track lacks proof/evidence maturity, it remains non-default and explicitly experimental.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:00.489590642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:58:25.643699612Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","ci","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:00.489590642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:23.181365188Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:25.698968900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:28.204818575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2392,"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Second polish-round correction:

- This forces the plan to validate the composed system users actually experience, including supported/experimental/forbidden combination matrices and incident drill artifacts.","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:47Z"}]}
